Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Complex A sophisticated cyber attack orchestrated by the expert group C0017 aims to infiltrate and disrupt critical energy infrastructure. The attackers employ a multi-stage approach, starting with extensive reconnaissance and culminating in significant impact operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Command & Control Server, Windows Registry, Network Traffic, Public IP/Network Block, Target System Artifact, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Response Metadata (Mechanism), Bash Script, Web Shell, Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism, Reg.exe Utility, Mimikatz Tool, Network Service Scanning, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), File Access (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, Process Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers conduct active scanning to map the network topology of the energy sector, identifying potential entry points and vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers establish command and control servers by acquiring infrastructure to facilitate communication with compromised systems.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit known vulnerabilities in public-facing web applications to gain initial access to the energy sector's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Once inside, the attackers execute malicious commands using the bash interpreter to further penetrate the system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain access, the attackers deploy a web shell on the compromised server, providing a persistent backdoor.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers escalate privileges to gain higher-level access within the compromised environment.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: To evade detection, the attackers manipulate registry settings to disable security features and logging.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers steal credentials using tools like Mimikatz to enable lateral movement throughout the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers map the internal network to discover valuable targets and plan lateral movements.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers utilize remote services like RDP or SSH to move laterally across the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from local systems to gather valuable information.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Data is transferred back to the attacker's controlled infrastructure via established command and control channels.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers inhibit system recovery efforts post-data exfiltration or disruption, ensuring maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: The complex cyber attack on the energy sector involves multiple stages, from initial reconnaissance to achieving significant impact. Expert attackers use a variety of techniques to gain access, maintain persistence, and ultimately disrupt operations while evading detection and inhibiting recovery efforts.
